# Badge

A span with utilities. Included so you can stop wondering whether the library has one.

```html
<span class="inline-flex items-center rounded-full bg-primary px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium text-primary-foreground">Default</span>

<span class="inline-flex items-center rounded-full bg-secondary px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium text-secondary-foreground">Secondary</span>

<span class="inline-flex items-center rounded-full border border-border px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium text-foreground">Outline</span>

<span class="inline-flex items-center rounded-full bg-destructive px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium text-white">Destructive</span>
```

## How it works

- One `<span>`, four utility sets. Use `<a>` instead if it links somewhere — same classes work.
- If the badge conveys status ("Failed", "Live"), don't rely on color alone — the text is
  the accessible part.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

None. This component uses only Baseline CSS/HTML — it works the same in every current browser.
